summary: Disambiguate OpenSCAD command-line options from CSS custom properties without weakening Appendix C enforcement in TypeScript, TSX, or CSS.

externalInputs.purpose: Inspect the exact hosted Web-check failure and confirm that only quoted OpenSCAD CLI options were being misclassified as CSS custom properties.

decisions.decision: Scan script source for custom properties only in var() expressions, CSSOM property methods, and quoted or unquoted object-property declarations while continuing to scan every custom-property-shaped token in CSS files.
decisions.rationale: A bare quoted token beginning with two hyphens is also the normal representation of a command-line option. Restricting script matches to CSS-bearing syntax removes false positives without allowing unknown Appendix C variables through the production CSS, CSS-in-JS, CSSOM, or style-object paths ScadMill uses or can introduce.

testEvidence.criterion: OpenSCAD CLI arguments do not fail the Appendix C gate, while real unknown custom properties in script and stylesheet contexts still fail it
testEvidence.test: Exercise quoted OpenSCAD CLI arguments alongside var(), CSSOM set/read, and style-object custom properties, then scan the complete production tree.

red.command: Hosted Web checks for e4551c02dc34eb7dbf7093441062dc7bac1dcebc; pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/theme/theme-css-variable-usage.test.ts
red.outcome: RED: hosted CI rejected seven valid OpenSCAD CLI option occurrences; the adversarial regression extension then failed 1 of 3 focused tests because CSSOM and quoted object-key custom properties were not yet detected.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/theme/theme-css-variable-usage.test.ts; pnpm.cmd exec biome check tests/application/theme/theme-css-variable-usage.test.ts
green.outcome: GREEN: 1 focused test file and all 3 tests passed in 657 ms; the focused Biome check examined the file with no fixes or diagnostics.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
